title: urania ravenala
side_effects: Urania ravenala can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, headaches, and dizziness due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Ingestion of large amounts may lead to more severe side effects, including vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. Prolonged use or misuse of the plant may also result in allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and rashes. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant can cause eye and respiratory irritation, which may be severe in individuals with pre-existing conditions.
